A special committee of the whole
council will meet tonight to con-
slider the proposition of passing a
new ordinance increasing the in
terest rate on the $780, 100 bridge
bonds authorized by the last cleo
ton from 4 to Az per cent. No
bids were received at 4 per cent.
1t is understood, though, that the
entire issue can be sold at Az
per cent.


The meeting will also take up
the question of hiring an expert
for the Monroe st. bridge, PROPOSE
tons from VIM. H. Burr of New
York and j. A. l.. Waddell of Kan
sas City, having been received.
The council is also expected to
authorize the board of public
works to enter into contracts with
the Washington Water Power Co.
Traction Co., 0. X. & n. and Spo
kane & International railroads fOl
| part payment of cost of new
bridges on Monroe St, Howard ST
and Washington st.
